response:
[SOPHIA HAS TWO SCIENCE TESTS, BIOLOGY AND CHEMISTRY, WITH 25 AND 20 QUESTIONS, RESPECTIVELY. SHE HAS 45 MINUTES TO COMPLETE THE BIOLOGY TEST AND 1 HOUR TO COMPLETE THE CHEMISTRY TEST. IF SOPHIA WANTS TO ALLOCATE THE SAME AMOUNT OF TIME FOR EACH QUESTION, HOW MANY MORE SECONDS CAN SHE SPEND ON EACH QUESTION IN THE CHEMISTRY TEST COMPARED TO THE BIOLOGY TEST?]

[CONVERT THE TIME FOR EACH TEST INTO SECONDS. FOR BIOLOGY, 45 MINUTES IS 2700 SECONDS. FOR CHEMISTRY, 1 HOUR IS 3600 SECONDS.]

[DIVIDE THE TOTAL TIME BY THE NUMBER OF QUESTIONS TO FIND THE TIME PER QUESTION. FOR BIOLOGY, 2700 SECONDS DIVIDED BY 25 QUESTIONS EQUALS 108 SECONDS PER QUESTION. FOR CHEMISTRY, 3600 SECONDS DIVIDED BY 20 QUESTIONS EQUALS 180 SECONDS PER QUESTION.]

[SUBTRACT THE TIME PER QUESTION FOR BIOLOGY FROM THE TIME PER QUESTION FOR CHEMISTRY. 180 SECONDS MINUS 108 SECONDS EQUALS 72 SECONDS.]

[SOPHIA CAN SPEND 72 MORE SECONDS ON EACH QUESTION IN THE CHEMISTRY TEST COMPARED TO THE BIOLOGY TEST.]
